title: Anatomy and Function of a Biological Neuron
description: A labeled diagram showing the key structural components of a neuron and the flow of information through it.
relationships: Dendrites receive electrical signals from other neurons
Signals are integrated in the Soma, which contains the Nucleus
If the signal exceeds a threshold, an action potential is sent down the Axon
The Myelin Sheath insulates the Axon to speed up signal propagation
The signal jumps between the gaps in the sheath, called Nodes of Ranvier
The signal reaches the Axon Terminals, triggering the release of neurotransmitters
Neurotransmitters cross the Synapse to stimulate the Dendrites of the next neuron
